name: Blackfish (SS-221)
events: 19 Oct 1942 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. John Frederick Davidson) departed from New London bound for the north-west coast of Africa. Blackfish was to take part in the landing in North Africa.

9 Nov 1942 Patrolling in the Dakar area of the central Atlantic, intercepted a French convoy with 3 freighters. USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Davidson) attacked, firing 2 torpedoes aimed at the sloop Commandant Bory in position 17°45'N, 14°50'W. The sloop reported a premature off the beam and no damage.
27 Nov 1942 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Davidson) ended her 1st war patrol at Rosneath, Scotland.
21 Dec 1942 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Davidson) departed from Rosneath, Scotland for her 2nd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the North Atlantic.

1 Jan 1943 In a signal timed 0656 A/1, the Flag Officer Submarines (F.O.S.) informed USS Shad (T/Cdr. E.J. MacGregor, 3rd, USN), USS Blackfish (T/Cdr. J.F. Davidson, USN), USS Herring (T/Cdr. R.W. Johnson, USN), USS Barb (T/Cdr. J.R. Waterman, USN) and HrMs Dolfijn (Lt.Cdr. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) of the following; Aircraft report one enemy merchant ship within 15 miles of position 45°45'N, 13°50'W, course 90°, speed 11 knots. Inward-bound blockade-runners are sometimes escorted by U-Boats.

In a signal timed 0719 A/1 the F.O.S. sent the following orders to the above listed submarines; My 0641 A/1. If not acting on aircraft homing signals submarines operate as follows; HrMs Dolfijn between parallels 46°20'N and 46°01'N. USS Blackfish between 46°01'N and 45°40'N. USS Herring between 45°40'N and 45°20'N. HMS Barb between 45°20'N and 45°01'N. All submarines making west as far as possible to ensure interception of the enemy in daylight.

In a signal timed 0909 A/1 the F.O.S. sent the following information to the submarines listed above; Estimated positions of our surface forces at 0800/1; 1) HMS Scylla (Capt. I.A.P. Macintyre, CBE, DSO, RN) in position 42°50'N, 16°01'W. Northbound convoy MKS 4 escorted by sloops and corvettes in position 45°40'N, 14°50'W. 2) HMS Scylla and three escorts of MKS 4 have been ordered to proceed to intercept and have been informed our submarines are expected to intercept east of 13°00'W. [The Senior Officer of the escort had detached two of the escorts instead of three, this were HMS Lulworth (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) C. Gwinner, DSO, RN) and HMCS Moosejaw (T/Lt. L.D. Quick, RCN).]

In a signal timed 1114 A/1, the F.O.S. sent the following information the the submarines listed above; My 0909 A/1, Paragraph 2, position, course and speed of HMS Lulworth and HMCS Moosejaw is 45° 37'N, 14°01'W, 090°, 15 knots.

In a signal timed 1151 A/1, the following orders were sent to the submarines listed; Estimate longitude enemy at 1200 A/1 was 12°30'W. If to westward of enemy USS Blackfish, USS Herring and USS Barb sweep on course 90° at best convenient speed.

In a signal timed 1247 A/1, the F.O.S. sent the following orders to the submarines listed; My 1151 A/1. USS Blackfish, USS Herring and USS Barb are to operate between limits of latitudes ordered in my signal timed 0719 A/1. Adjust course as necessary.

In a signal timed 1624 A/1, the following was sent by the F.O.S.; 1) Estimate Dolfijn's position at 2000 A/1 as being 46°05'N, 12°30'W. 2) Dolfijn is to proceed forthwith on route 'Red', diving by day, passing through position 'C' (42°59'N, 12°26'W at 0600 A/3.

In a signal timed 1714 A/1, the following information and orders were sent by the F.O.S.; Aircraft report position, course and speed of the enemy at 1634 A/1 as being 45°12'N, 10°48'W, 090°, 12 knots. USS Shad and HrMs Dolfijn proceed to intercept.
In a signal timed 1830 A/1 to the Admiralty, HMS Scylla reported to have sunk the inbound enemy blockade breaker.

In a signal timed 1923 A/1, the F.O.S. sent the following information / orders; 1) Enemy was sunk by HMS Scylla at 1830 A/1. 2) Submarines return forthwith to original patrol. USS Shad off Bilbao, USS Barb off Vigo, USS Blackfish in zone 'K 501' and USS Herring off Ferrol. (1)
18 Jan 1943 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Davidson) ended her 2nd war patrol at Rosneath, Scotland.
1 Feb 1943 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Davidson) departed from Rosneath, Scotland for her 3rd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Bay of Biscay.

19 Feb 1943 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Davidson) attacked a section of German Vorpostenboote (auxiliary patrol craft), torpedoed and sank V 408 / Haltenbank (432 GRT) north of Bilbao, Spain in position 43°30'N, 02°54'W, but is then counterattacked, depth charged and damaged by V 404.
22 Feb 1943 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Davidson) ended her 3rd war patrol at Plymouth, England.

24 Mar 1943 Around 1400 A/24, HMS Una (Lt. J.D. Martin, RN) joined HMS P 556 (Lt.Cdr. F.H. Sherwood, RCNVR) and USS Blackfish (T/Cdr. J.F. Davidson, USN) which came from Falmouth. They were escorted by HMS Lundy (T/Lt. S.J. Lavis, RNR).
Around 1820 A/24, HMS Lundy parted company and shortly afterwards HMS La Capricieuse (Lt.Cdr. G.W. Dobson, RNR) joined. (2)
5 Apr 1943 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Davidson) departed from base for her 4th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Norwegian Sea.

12 May 1943 HMS Usurper (Lt. D.R.O. Mott, DSC, RN), USS Barb (Lt.Cdr. N. Lucker, Jr., USN) and USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Davidson, USN) departed Lerwick for Holy Loch. They were escorted by HMS La Capricieuse (Lt.Cdr. G.W. Dobson, RNR).
14 May 1943 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Davidson) ended her 4th war patrol at Rosneath, Scotland.
4 Jul 1943 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. Eliot Olsen) ended her 5th war patrol.
26 Jul 1943 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. E. Olsen) arrived at New London.
14 Aug 1943 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Davidson, USN) departed New London, Connecticut for the Panama Canal Zone.
21 Aug 1943 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Davidson, USN) arrived in the Panama Canal Zone from New London, Connecticut.
19 Oct 1943 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Davidson) departed from Brisbane, Australia for her 6th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol north of New Guinea.
23 Nov 1943 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Davidson) torpedoed and sank the Japanese transport ship Yamato Maru No.2 (439 GRT) between New Guinea and Palau in position 02°28'N, 140°06'E.

24 Nov 1943 Patrolling in the Palau area, at about 2300 hrs Blackfish attacked a target on radar without visual contacts, firing 6 torpedoes and claiming 2 hits and a sinking. The target, estimated by the radar officer to have been a 4500-ton vessel, has never been identified and no Japanese report found to confirm any damage or sinking at this time.
4 Dec 1943 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Davidson) ended her 6th war patrol at Milne Bay.
24 Dec 1943 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Davidson) departed from Milne Bay for her 7th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off the Bismarck Archipelago.
29 Dec 1943 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. John F. Davidson) refuels at Tulagi, and departed from the next day.

8 Jan 1944 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Davidson) makes a periscope attack against an oiler, estimated at 4500 tgr, firing 3 torpedoes and claiming 2 hits for damage. There is no confirmation of actual hits or target identification.
16 Jan 1944 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Davidson) torpedoed and sank the Japanese troop transport Kaika Maru (2087 GRT) about 275 nautical miles south-west of Truk in position 03°50'N, 148°41'E.
In the same attack, Blackfish also claims damaging a "4500-ton tanker" with 1 hit out of 4 torpedoes. This is unconfirmed.

3 Feb 1944 On patrol north of New Ireland, position 01°36'S, 150°09'E, USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Davidson) fires 6 torpedoes at a "1800-ton destroyer", claiming 2 hits and damage to the target. This is unconfirmed.
13 Feb 1944 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Davidson) ended her 7th war patrol at Brisbane.
1 Mar 1944 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. Robert Frederick Sellars) departed from Brisbane for her 8th war patrol, and was ordered to patrol off New Guinea.

27 Apr 1944 Off Tual island (Moluccas), position 05°35'S, 133°17'E, Blackfish fires 4 torpedoed on radar bearings at a target identified as a " 3700-ton light cruiser or destroyer", apparently an Akizuki-class large AA destroyer. The sub claims one possible hit and damage. The target and results of the attack are, again, unidentified and unconfirmed.
19 May 1944 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. R.F. Sellars) ended her 8th war patrol at Pearl Harbor. She is sent to San Francisco for a major overhaul.
27 May 1944 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. R.F. Sellars) arrived at San Francisco.
31 Aug 1944 With her overhaul completed USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. R.F. Sellars) departed from San Francisco for Pearl Harbor.

17 Sep 1944 USS Blackfin (Cdr. G.H. Laird, Jr., USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor together with USS Le Hardy (Lt.Cdr. E.L. Holtz, USNR), USS Allen (Lt. J.A. Rowe, USNR) and USS Blackfish (Cdr. R.F. Sellars, USN).

19 Sep 1944 USS Seadragon (Cdr. J.H. Ashley, Jr., USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor together with USS Le Hardy (Lt.Cdr. E.L. Holtz, USNR), USS Tisdale (Lt.Cdr. J.S. Hatfield, USNR) and USS Blackfish (Cdr. R.F. Sellars, USN).
23 Sep 1944 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. R.F. Sellars) departed from Pearl Harbor for Saipan.
3 Oct 1944 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. R.F. Sellars) arrived at Saipan.
4 Oct 1944 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. R.F. Sellars) departed from Saipan for her 9th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off Formosa.
17 Nov 1944 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. R.F. Sellars) ended her 9th war patrol at Saipan.
1 Jan 1945 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. R.F. Sellars) departed from Midway for her 10th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the South China Sea.
23 Jan 1945 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. R.F. Sellars) attacked a sailing vessel with her deck gun, claiming damage to the vessel.

30 Jan 1945 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. R.F. Sellars) sank a large Japanese sampan with gunfire in the South China Sea in position 21°37'N, 115°54'E. She also claims scoring hits on a second, larger, sampan but not a sinking.
1 Feb 1945 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. R.F. Sellars) sank three Japanese sampans with gunfire in the South China Sea in position 21°58'N, 115°39'E.
21 Feb 1945 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. R.F. Sellars) ended her 10th war patrol at Guam.

17 Mar 1945 USS Blackfish (T/Cdr. R.F. Sellars, USN), USS Burrfish (T/Lt.Cdr. M.H. Lytle, USN) and USS Bang (T/Lt.Cdr. O.W. Bagby, Jr., USN) conducted exercises off Guam with USS Doherty (Lt.Cdr. W.J. O'Brien, USNR), USS Baron (Lt.Cdr. W.M. Dickerson, USNR) and USS Greenlet (Lt.Cdr. J.T. Hodgson, Jr., USNR).
21 Mar 1945 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. R.F. Sellars) departed from Guam for her 11th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the South China Sea.

21 Mar 1945 Around 1315 K/21, USS Tigrone (T/Cdr. H. Cassedy, USN), USS Bullhead (T/Cdr. W.T. Griffith, USN), USS Seahorse (T/Cdr. H.H. Greer, Jr., USN) and USS Blackfish (T/Cdr. R.F. Sellars, USN) departed Guam for their patrol area. [For USS Blackfish this is her eleventh war patrol.]
They were to patrol in the South China Sea or East China Sea (USS Seahorse).
They were escorted out by USS PC-1256 (Lt. B.W. Partridge, Jr., USNR) until 2000 K/21.
The submarines remained in company until 25 March. (3)
10 May 1945 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. R.F. Sellars) ended her 11th war patrol at Pearl Harbor.
14 Jun 1945 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. Robert Charles Gillette) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 12th war patrol, and was ordered to patrol in the East China and Yellow Seas.
14 Aug 1945 USS Blackfish (Lt.Cdr. R.C. Gillette) ended her 12th war patrol at Guam.
